Title: Silverburn AU: Tesco Grand Opening & Upcoming Construction

Thread Started by ShopaholicSuzie on June 15, 2002

ShopaholicSuzie:
Hey everyone on PollokShoppers! 😍 It’s official - the new Tesco at Silverburn opened its doors today, and it’s HUUUGE! 🛒✨ But that’s not all, I overheard the construction crew talking about breaking ground on the rest of the centre soon. Finally, we’ll have some high-end shopping right on our doorstep. Just had to share the excitement!!!

BargainHunter99:
Yeah, the Tesco is nice and all, but have you heard the gossip about Farmfoods, Gala Bingo, and Greggs? Word is they’re resisting moving out of the old Pollok Centre. They say they’re ‘too low class’ for the shiny new Silverburn. Honestly, can you believe that? 😒

LocalGreggsFan:
Gala Bingo and Farmfoods, I get, but Greggs? C’mon, it’s a staple! There’s nothing ‘low class’ about a delicious sausage roll. Plus, they seem to be working out a deal to fit into the new aesthetic so fingers crossed 🤞🥖

TrendyTracy:
This is the kind of snobbery that splits communities IMO. Remember when we had all those independent shops before the big chains swooped in? Silverburn should reflect ALL of us, not just those who want to flash their cash. 🙄 #DiversityInRetail

ShopaholicSuzie:
@TrendyTracy, I totally get where you’re coming from, but think of the job opportunities! Plus, we deserve a bit of glamour too, don’t you think? Why should we always have to travel into the city for the fancy stores? 💅🏻✨

PollokPurist:
I’m not thrilled about this. Tesco is just the beginning. What’s next, designer boutiques where the community centre used to be? Don’t get me wrong, I like progress, but not at the cost of our identity. #KeepPollokAuthentic

SilverburnInsider:
Insider scoop here – the management is working hard to make sure the centre has a mix of shops. The idea is to cater to everyone. Greggs has a strong presence in the community, so I wouldn’t count them out just yet!

DealDiva:
Speaking of Tesco, anyone knows if they’re doing any opening specials? Maybe even a BOGO deal on pizza or something? I could do with saving a few quid this week 😅🍕

ModeratorNotice:
Let’s keep the conversation respectful, folks! Remember, change can be daunting, but it can also bring new opportunities. Let’s wait and see what the full plans for Silverburn entail before jumping to conclusions. Keep sharing your thoughts and insights!

VoteForYourShops:
Hey everyone, there’s talk about a survey being circulated by the developers. If you want a say in which shops make it into the final lineup, now’s the time to speak up! 📝🏬

TheSilverburnAU thread would continue to accumulate responses, reflecting the diverse opinions and speculations of the Pollok community. Some users would remain skeptical, others optimistic, and all would be keeping a close eye on the evolution of their local shopping landscape.

Title: Silverburn AU: New Beginnings & Lingering Echoes of the Old Pollok Centre

Thread Started by ShopaholicSuzie on October 12, 2003

ShopaholicSuzie:
Hello PollokShoppers! 🎉 The moment so many of us have been waiting for has finally arrived - Silverburn is officially open! It’s got everything - fashion outlets, eateries, a cinema. It’s a dream come true! 🛍️🎬 But have you seen the old Pollok Centre? It’s a bit of a sight with some spots wrapped in wallpaper with artsy frames, a painted sky, and the flashy Silverburn logo. Halfway transformation, I guess? What are your thoughts?

BargainHunter99:
Drove past the old centre today, and it’s surreal. The wallpaper thing is odd. Feels like they’re stuck between wanting to preserve the past and pushing forward. Not sure how I feel about it… 😕

LocalGreggsFan:
Glad to report that Greggs is thriving in Silverburn! 😁 But you should see the wallpaper over at the old Pollok Centre; it’s like a giant art project. Weird to see the place dressed up as something it’s not. Anyone else feeling a little nostalgic?

TrendyTracy:
The old centre with the wallpaper looks like a plaster over a wound. It’s artsy, sure, but also a bit sad. Feels like a token gesture to ‘beautify’ what they haven’t torn down yet… 🎨🤔 #MixedFeelings

PollokPurist:
Saw the new Silverburn and wow – it’s impressive, can’t deny it. But that wallpaper on the old centre - it’s like they’re trying to hide our history. I mean, come on, a painted sky? We know it’s not the limit, so let’s not pretend it is. 🌌 #PreserveOurPast

SilverburnInsider:
The wallpaper covering is a temporary measure, folks. It’s meant to soften the transition and bring an element of Silverburn’s look to the old site while future plans are being finalized. There’s a bit of a symbolic merging happening so bear with us!

DealDiva:
Anyone else more interested in the Silverburn sales than the wallpaper drama? Let’s focus on the positive, like discounts! Though I must admit, catching a glimpse of the old centre dressed up in its ‘Sunday best’ is a bit comical. 😂 #SilverLinings

ModeratorNotice:
It’s clear that the community has strong feelings about the change. It’s okay to reminisce and hold onto the things that mean a lot to us. Remember to also give a chance to new beginnings. And yes, please keep it about shopping, deals, and community updates! 🛍️❤️

VoteForYourShops:
While Silverburn has been stealing the spotlight, let’s not forget we can still shape what’s left of the old Pollok Centre’s future. Keep sending your opinions to the developers; they’re listening. Let’s make sure we get a space that serves everyone! 📣🏢

As time passes, the community would continue to adjust to the changes. Silverburn’s gleaming presence would starkly contrast with the remnants of the old Pollok Centre shrouded in symbolic covers. Discussions would oscillate between excitement about the new and fond memories of the old, forming a tapestry as complex and diverse as the people it serves

Title: Silverburn Introduces the Future of Shopping with New Advertising Screens and a Dazzling Mascot Named Silver!

Date: October 26, 2005

Hey there, Glasgow shoppers and tech aficionados!

Big news! Silverburn, the shopping centre that’s fast becoming Glasgow’s retail magnet, has just announced an innovative approach that’s set to redefine our shopping experiences. Picture this: You’re strolling through the wide, gleaming halls of Silverburn, surrounded by your favourite brands, and then something catches your eye—a captivating video screen, not with the usual fare of static ads, but with idyllic piers stretching into serene waters, vibrant balloons gliding through clear skies, and Frisbees sailing in a dance with the breeze. It’s not a daydream; it’s the new, mesmerizing advertising screens introduced by Silverburn!

Designed to enhance the visual landscape and bring a burst of creativity into the heart of our consumer culture, these screens are unlike anything we’ve seen before in Glasgow, or anywhere in the UK for that matter. The promotional idents that adorn these screens have been masterfully crafted by none other than Proud Creative, the design wizards known for their innovative touch.

As you wander through the avenues of Silverburn, you’ll notice that each video, whether it’s those peaceful piers or the playful balloons, features the shopping centre’s logo discreetly nestled in the corner—a gentle reminder of where you are amongst the captivating content. It’s an advertising revolution that’s subtle yet impactful, enhancing the ambiance without overshadowing the shopping experience, These 20 second idents play in-between each ad.

But wait till you hear about what happens at closing time! In a move that truly embraces the digital age, Silverburn goes into a tranquil mode a half-hour before the doors close. The screens transition to a mesmerizing loop of gently moving colored lines. It’s almost a digital version of the Northern Lights, shimmering across the concourse’s skies, ushering in a calming end to a day of retail therapy.

In addition to this aesthetic overhaul, Silverburn has unveiled a character that’s destined to become Glasgow’s new loveable icon—meet Silver! Silver, a gleaming, animated mascot, represents the bright and shiny essence of the Silverburn shopping experience. With endearing antics and an unforgettable shine, Silver will guide shoppers to special deals, entertain the little ones, and be the shining embodiment of Silverburn’s commitment to making shopping not just a chore, but a delightful journey.

As Silverburn continues to push boundaries and innovate, it’s clear that these latest introductions are just the beginning. The shopping centre is not just a place to buy goods—it’s becoming the backdrop for a dynamic fusion of commerce, digital artistry, and community engagement.

For those who haven’t witnessed this transformation yet, head down to Silverburn and prepare to be mesmerized by the seamless blend of shopping and visual enchantment. Who knew retail could be so revolutionary?

Keep shining bright, Glasgow, and let Silverburn’s new spark lead the way!

Until next time,
[Your Name]

Blog Title: Christmas 2008 Brings Gooey Goodbye to Silver and a Morning Surprise!

Date: December 3, 2008

Ho, ho, ho, Silverburn shoppers and early birds alike!

Christmas came early this year with a morning mishap that had us chuckling over our cornflakes. It’s time to talk about Silverburn’s latest ad campaign, the new mascots on the block, and a fond farewell to a shiny friend.

Gloopy & Glitter Steal the Morning Show

Let me set the scene: It’s a brisk December morning, the kind where you’re snuggled in your PJs, a cup of hot cocoa in hand, and your favourite morning show playing on the telly. Then, just as the show reaches its climax—BAM!—the screen cuts to Gloopy and Glitter’s gooey grand entrance. If you’re one to indulge in a spot of Toonattik with your tots or alone (no judgement here!), you’ll have witnessed this firsthand. Whoops? More like, Wow!

Not an ideal debut time, you say? We agree, but this extraterrestrial escapade into the wee hours of children’s programming was more than just a faux pas—it was a brilliant strategy. The kids are hooked, parents are bemused, and we’re all suddenly invested in the love life of two amorphous blobs. Talk about effective advertising!

Silver’s Shiny Send-Off

Now, it wasn’t just Gloopy and Glitter’s surprise premiere that had the rumour mill spinning. It’s the bittersweet news that Silverburn’s long-standing mascot, Silver, is set to retire by the year’s end. Yes, the gleaming guardian of the galaxy of shops we’ve grown to love is taking a final bow, making room for our new gelatinous duo to spread their glittery charm.

Let’s raise a glass to Silver, the metallic mate who’s seen us through many a shopping spree. Though we’re sad to see him go, it’s heartening to know he’s leaving us in the capable… blobs?.. of Gloopy and Glitter.

A Laugh at the Launch Timing

Back to the premiere that sparked the buzz—turns out, someone at the ad agency must have decided that all the evening slots were too mainstream. “Let’s show the world our boldness,” they declared, “let’s air it when they least expect it!” And thus, a looming figure in the ad world is now affectionately dubbed ‘the morning maverick’. Wearing this title with unintended pride, they gave us a launch to remember and a story for years to come.

Embracing Our Gooey Future

As we chuckle at the enthusiastic aliens taking over our breakfast TV slots, let’s also embrace this change with open arms (and maybe a bit of hand sanitizer—for the goo). Silverburn Shopping Centre is setting the stage for a new era: one that promises to be filled with humour, unexpected joys, and shopping experiences that are out of this world.

So, here’s to a Christmas season with a twist! Expect the unexpected, find humour in the little things, and may the gooey goodness of Gloopy and Glitter warm your hearts. Silver might be stepping out of the spotlight, but the future of Silverburn shines bright…or shall we say, sparkles?

Merry Christmas, shoppers! 🎄✨

With a twinkle in my eye,
[Your Name]
Silverburn’s Enthusiastic Shopper and Morning Show Commentator

Character Description: Gloopy

Appearance:
Gloopy is an amorphous, enchanting alien with a body of translucent teal that glistens as if reflecting the depths of an ocean world. Gloopy’s form is ever-shifting, his gelatinous, blob-like structure giving him an air of gentle fluidity. Depending on his mood, he can shift from a bouncy ball to a languid puddle, often adorned with soft glimmers of bioluminescent speckles that twinkle like distant stars. His two large, expressive eyes gleam with a friendly, sapphire-blue glow that instantly endears him to all.

Personality:
Gloopy is the embodiment of chill and calm. He is carefree, always taking life at a leisurely pace (or float, as is more accurate for his kind). This gooey alien is innately kind, with an empathetic heart that resonates with the feelings of others. Despite his lack of a mouth, he has a way of communicating warmth and laughter through his shimmering form, and his bubbly personality makes him the soul of Silverburn’s extraterrestrial duo. Curious and playful, Gloopy loves to interact with the centre’s visitors, especially the children, making him a lovable part of many family memories.

—

Character Description: Glitter

Appearance:
Glitter stands as Gloopy’s vibrant counterpart with a sparkling, amorphous form that cascades through shades of radiant fuchsia and vivacious purple. Tiny specks of reflective gold are suspended in her body, giving her the appearance of a walking galaxy full of mysterious allure. Glitter can change shape at will, often settling into soft, rounded figures that exude both majesty and charm. Her one oversized, glistening eye catches the light, radiating an inviting and playful warmth in a rich magenta hue.

Personality:
Glitter is the adventurer of the pair, full of enthusiasm and an insatiable curiosity about the world around her. She embodies the joy of discovery and cannot resist the allure of an exciting new experience or a heartwarming interaction. Her vivacious nature is contagious, and she has a knack for spreading happiness and laughter wherever she goes. Though she communicates through harmonic vibrations that she emits, her intentions are always clear—Glitter wants to make the centre a brighter place for everyone. Dynamic and compassionate, she is the spark that keeps the spirit of Silverburn shining all year round.

Together, Gloopy and Glitter are more than just Silverburn mascots—they are a delightful reminder of the unexpected and joyous moments that make the holiday seasons at Silverburn unique and memorable. Their contrasting personalities and appearances blend in perfect harmony, representing the diverse community that comes together at the shopping centre to celebrate the festive times. Greetings fellow shoppers and extraterrestrial enthusiasts!

Today marks the dawn of a new era at Silverburn Shopping Centre, and it is with both a touch of nostalgia and a wave of excitement that I bid a fond farewell to Silver, the shimmering mascot who has lit up the halls of our beloved centre with his twinkle and charm. But as we close one chapter, we eagerly turn the pages to begin another. It’s time to stretch out our arms—or tentacles—and welcome the cosmic presence of two gooey new icons: Gloopy and Glitter!

Riding on the comet tail of Silver’s departure, Gloopy and Glitter arrive at Silverburn with a burst of interstellar energy and a kaleidoscope of colour that could rival the Aurora Borealis itself. These amorphous alien friends bring with them a sense of wonder and an invitation to explore the endless possibilities of the universe, much like the endless shopping opportunities at Silverburn.

Both Gloopy and Glitter, with their sparkles and fluidity, embody the dynamic and ever-evolving experience that Silverburn offers to its patrons. They represent the melting pot of culture, fashion, and leisure that our shopping centre is known for. With Gloopy’s tranquil teal vibes and Glitter’s radiant fuchsia flare, they make an otherworldly pair that are impossible to ignore.

Their introduction is not a moment too soon. As Silverburn embraces the future, the switch from our metallic buddy to these lovable blobs symbolizes the shopping centre’s commitment to innovation and excitement. What’s more fascinating is the inclusive narrative behind their existence—Gloopy and Glitter are not bound by shape or form, much like Silverburn’s diverse offerings are not limited to any single style or trend.

As we welcome these enchanting mascots, let’s celebrate the magic they are set to sprinkle throughout our shopping escapades. Whether you’re a child marveling at the sight of Gloopy’s soothing, playful dances or an adult taken aback by Glitter’s adventurous aura, these mascots are sure to become an integral part of the Silverburn community.

The excitement isn’t just confined to appearances, either. Buzz has it that Gloopy and Glitter are slated to be the faces of Silverburn’s very own kids’ club, set to launch this February. I envision days filled with laughter and engagement, where families can connect and create memories that are as vivid and joyful as our new alien friends.

So as we say goodbye to Silver, I encourage all Silverburn visitors to approach Gloopy and Glitter with openness and curiosity. In the great expanse of our shopping galaxy, they are the bright stars guiding us toward fun-filled adventures. It is truly an exciting time to be part of Silverburn’s cosmic dance, and Gloopy and Glitter are poised to lead the way.

Here’s to a future full of discovery, fun, and perhaps a little bit of space-age slime!

—Case Study: Designing Gloopy and Glitter for Silverburn Shopping Centre

Background:
In early 2008, Silverburn Shopping Centre, aiming to enhance its brand and encapsulate its spirit in an engaging new way, sought to create unique and innovative mascots. They commissioned the same creative minds behind their successful 2007 Christmas ads to fulfill the brief.

The Brief:
The challenge presented to the creative team was to conceptualize and design “abstract creatures that embody the centre’s spirit.” These characters needed to be versatile, attractive to both children and adults, and adaptable to various marketing campaigns. Furthermore, these mascots were expected to be fully realized by October, with an animated Christmas ad to be produced in November. Silverburn planned to introduce the characters during the Christmas season and then fully launch them as centre ambassadors in February of the following year.

Design Process:
Brainstorming Stage:
The creative team began by diving into the essence of Silverburn—its blend of fashion, entertainment, and dining, and its importance as a community hub. The goal was to distil these characteristics into a set of mascots that would personify the centre’s vibrant and dynamic nature.

Conceptualization:
Abstract concepts were explored to avoid limiting the characters to any specific demographic or cultural representations. The team focused on attributes such as fluidity, brightness, and playfulness—components they felt resonated with Silverburn’s brand identity.

Character Creation:
From the pool of concepts, two characters began to take shape—Gloopy, representing tranquility and inclusivity, and Glitter, exuding excitement and discovery. Gloopy was designed as a serene, teal blob that could shift shape, while Glitter took on a more energetic, sparkly fuchsia form. Both characters had a malleable nature, enabling them to engage in all forms of centre activities.

Design Refinement:
The characters went through several design iterations, receiving input from focus groups composed of Silverburn shoppers. Their feedback was instrumental in perfecting the characters’ appearances and ensuring their broad appeal.

Animation:
To meet the tight deadline, animators worked closely with the designers to bring Gloopy and Glitter to life. Their first animated appearance, the Christmas ad, was storyboarded and developed to showcase their personalities and interaction with the centre’s environment.

Christmas Introduction and Full Launch:
Adhering to the project timeline, Gloopy and Glitter were successfully introduced to the public through a heartwarming Christmas ad that aired as planned. They were depicted spreading holiday cheer around the centre, captivating shoppers with their charm. The animated ad was well-received, paving the way for the full launch of the characters in February 2009.

Outcomes:
Gloopy and Glitter quickly became Silverburn’s beloved mascots, the abstract embodiments of the shopping centre’s lively and inclusive spirit. Shopper engagement increased, and the mascots became key to the centre’s brand identity, featuring in ongoing marketing campaigns, special events, and community activities. Their success has established them as iconic figures, enduring in shopper’s minds and contributing significantly to Silverburn’s brand image and appeal.

Reflection:
The development and success of Gloopy and Glitter demonstrate the importance of well-defined brand mascots. With careful design, attention to customer feedback, and strategic marketing integration, abstract characters can effectively capture and communicate a centre’s spirit, enhancing overall customer experience and loyalty.

Blog Title: Glitter’s Behind-the-Scenes Goodbye: Crafting Closure Messages at Silverburn

Date: February 14, 2014

Hello, my bright beacons of the Silverburn universe!

Feels like a while since my last post, I think it was about Sparkle time—our little secret about how Gloopy and I manage our, ahem, natural processes with poofy sparkles or a quick twinkle.

Today I’m swapping twinkles for transcriptions as I, Glitter, come clean about those poignant parting words you saw throughout our beloved Silverburn. That’s right, the closure notices for the stores at the old end of the centre were composed by yours truly—and my gelatinous genius of a companion, Gloopy, of course!

Now, you might be wondering, why didn’t the stores whip up their own swan songs? Well, darlings, during times of transition, it’s important to unify the message and speak to every heart that beats for Silverburn. Who better to thread this needle than the shopping centre’s own stars?

Crafting each closure message was no small feat. I wanted to capture the soul of every store, mix it with a dash of hope for the future and a sprinkle of Silverburn magic. From Gala Bingo’s cosmic farewell to Farmfoods’ final call, every word was dipped in respect and reverence for the legacy left behind.

Why speak through stores, you ask? To give a voice to the inanimate, to spark that human connection we all cherish—a connection that extends beyond transactions and into the realm of shared memories. And yes, with a dollop of our signature silliness, too.

For Farmfoods’ address, not only did I script the words, but we pulled strings to have the CEO voice them—how’s that for gravitas? Yet amidst the gravity, we laid a bed of music beneath the spoken notice. A composed symphony to elevate the message beyond mere words, echoing our sentiment across the shopping halls.

Oh, and before you picture Gloopy and me hunched over a desk with a quill and ink… pause that thought! We took a more modern approach, collaborating with the brilliant minds at Silverburn to blend our blobby perspectives with their savvy insights.

As we mixed nostalgia with novelty, our goal was clear: to set the stage for what’s next while honouring where we’ve been—a delicate balance, like walking on a beam of moonlight, wouldn’t you agree?

So, as we look ahead to the expansion and all the shiny new adventures that await, let’s not forget to cast a smile back at the memories we shared. The echoes of laughter in the bingo hall, the taste of your favourite blue Lagoon dish, the warmth of a freshly baked Greggs pastry—cherish these moments, for they are the foundation upon which we build our tomorrow.

Remember, change can be a mixture of sweet and sour, but with a little bit of glitter, even goodbyes can sparkle.

Until next time, keep shining and shopping,

**Blog Title: From Silver Linings to Gooey Beginnings: The Evolution of Silverburn's Mascots**

**Date: June 27, 2009**

Hello, Silverburn shoppers and enthusiasts!

It's been a whirlwind of sparkles and feelings here at Silverburn as we embrace the significant shift from our beloved metallic mascot, Silver, to the enchanting duo, Gloopy and Glitter. It feels like yesterday when Silver was the one leading the seasons' celebrations and guiding us through aisles of adventure and discount delights. However, as with all great tales, evolution is not only natural—it's essential. Today, I want to share with you the reasons behind this endearing transition and what it signifies for our vibrant community.

**Why Say Goodbye to Silver?**

Silver, with his gleaming presence, has been more than a mascot; he's been a beacon of guidance, a shiny embodiment of our commitment to providing a dazzling shopping experience. Over time, though, as Silverburn has grown and transformed, the essence of what we represent has shifted. Our horizons have expanded to not only provide a space for shopping but to also create a community where imagination, engagement, and innovation thrive.

Silver, static and reflective as he was, symbolized a phase of Silverburn that was, albeit golden, limited in its ability to interact, adapt, and evolve with the dynamism that now defines our space. Enter Gloopy and Glitter—the decision to transition from Silver to these two galactic companions wasn't made lightly.

**The Birth of Gloopy and Glitter: A New Era**

The introduction of Gloopy and Glitter was initiated to embody the fluidity, vibrancy, and endless possibilities that Silverburn has embraced. These characters were conceptualized to interact with each other—and with you—in ways that go beyond the physical dimensions of the shopping centre. Their ability to change shape, colour, and even mood mirrors the endless experiences that await in Silverburn.

Gloopy, with his serene hues and calming presence, represents the welcoming, inclusive atmosphere we strive to maintain. Meanwhile, Glitter, in her radiant, effervescent glory, embodies the excitement and discovery that comes with each visit. Together, they signal a departure from the traditional and a leap into a realm where shopping is not just an activity but an adventure.

**Why Change Was Necessary**

Change, as they say, is the only constant. As Silverburn has evolved to incorporate more digital interfaces, engaging events, and a broader spectrum of stores and services, it became apparent that our mascot(s) needed to reflect this new dynamic. Gloopy and Glitter, with their digital origins and adaptable nature, are perfectly poised to lead us into this era of heightened interactivity and community-centric experiences.

Moreover, this transition speaks to our dedication to keeping the magic of Silverburn alive—by ensuring that it evolves with the times, remains relevant, and continues to enchant and excite all who step through our doors.

**Looking Ahead**

As we bid a fond farewell to Silver, we warmly welcome Gloopy and Glitter into the Silverburn family. This change signifies more than just a new visual identity; it's a renewal of our promise to you—a promise to always innovate, inspire, and include.

We invite you to join us in this new chapter, filled with gooey smiles and glittery paths, as we continue to explore what it means to be a part of the ever-expanding universe of Silverburn. Together with Gloopy and Glitter, our journey is just beginning, and the possibilities are as limitless as their shapes.

Until next time, keep sparkling,

Write a follow up saying when this change was began internally and when it fully completed

**Blog Title: Reflecting on the Journey: From Silver to Sparkles**

**Date: December 10, 2009**

Dear Silverburn Community,

As the year draws to a close and Gloopy and Glitter illuminate our halls with their whimsical charm, I find myself reflecting on the journey of transformation that has taken place within Silverburn. From the conceptual farewell of Silver to today’s vibrant embrace of our gooey mascots, it’s been a path of innovation, nostalgia, and community spirit. I thought it might be enlightening to share a bit about the timeline of this transition, marking when the change began internally and when it was fully actualized in the eyes of our beloved shoppers.

**The Genesis of Change**

The initial discussions about transitioning from our stalwart mascot Silver to a new representation started in the spring of 2008. This was a period of introspection for us at Silverburn, as we sought to align our identity more closely with the evolving dynamics of our community and the broader retail experience. The decision was not made in haste; it emerged from numerous strategy sessions, creative workshops, and heartfelt conversations about who we are and who we aspire to be for our patrons.

By the summer of 2008, the decision had been solidified internally, marking the beginning of our journey towards embracing Gloopy and Glitter. It was a collaborative effort that involved every facet of the Silverburn team, ensuring that the transition would be seamless and meaningful.

**Designing the Future**

The latter half of 2008 was dedicated to the design and conceptualization phase. Gloopy and Glitter were born out of a desire to encapsulate the essence of Silverburn in a form that could interact, evolve, and spark imagination. This phase was meticulous, involving not just creative minds but also the valuable input of our community through focus groups and feedback sessions.

By early 2009, Gloopy and Glitter were ready to be introduced to the world, but not before we gave Silver a fitting tribute. His send-off was planned with care, celebrating the role he played and signaling the dawn of a new epoch.

**The Public Embrace**

February 2009 marked the official debut of Gloopy and Glitter to the public. It was both an exciting and poignant time, as we witnessed the culmination of months of hard work and the beginning of a new chapter. The unveiling was met with an outpouring of warmth and enthusiasm, confirming that Gloopy and Glitter were the right champions for the next phase of Silverburn’s journey.

**Full Circle**

The transition reached its full completion in the summer of 2009, with Gloopy and Glitter fully integrated into all aspects of the Silverburn experience. From branding and marketing to events and daily interactions, they had become intrinsic to the identity of Silverburn.

Looking back, the journey from Silver to Gloopy and Glitter was one of growth, creativity, and community engagement. It was a transition that spoke to our commitment to staying relevant and resonant with those we serve.

As we continue to forge ahead, we do so with the knowledge that change is a constant companion on the path to excellence. Gloopy and Glitter are more than mascots; they are symbols of our adaptability, our imagination, and our unwavering commitment to creating a shopping environment that is vibrant, inclusive, and ever-evolving.

Thank you for being part of this journey. Here’s to countless more adventures together, under the watchful eyes of Gloopy and Glitter.

Warmly,

**Blog Title: Turning the Page: From Magnetic Attraction to Animated Amusement with Gloopy & Glitter**

**Date: April 15, 2016**

Greetings, Silverburn shoppers and enthusiasts,

In the ever-evolving landscape of Silverburn, our commitment to bringing you an engaging and memorable shopping experience remains unwavering. Part of that commitment involves refreshing our approach to how we animate the heart and soul of Silverburn – quite literally! Today, I take you behind the scenes of a significant transition in our visual storytelling: the retirement of the "Magnetic" set and the birth of a new era with Gloopy & Glitter at the helm.

**A Magnetic Past**

Introduced with much fanfare, the "Magnetic" series of live-action idents – showcasing everyday objects like chairs and balloons mysteriously moving as if drawn by an unseen force – captured the imaginations of visitors young and old. These idents, embodying the magnetic allure of Silverburn's diverse attractions, became fixtures of our visual identity. However, as the digital age dawned more brightly, a portion of the "Magnetic" set was phased out in 2013 to make way for new innovations.

**Embracing Change**

Despite the charm and nostalgia of the "Magnetic" series, we recognized the need for a fresher approach that could resonate more deeply with today's audience. In May 2015, the decision was officially made to retire the remaining "Magnetic" idents. This choice was not made lightly but came from a desire to innovate and reflect the dynamic spirit of Silverburn more accurately.

The year 2015 was not solely about bidding farewell to an old friend but also about exciting new beginnings. Silverburn collaborated with a renowned animation studio to breathe life into two characters who have quickly become synonymous with joy and mischief at Silverburn: Gloopy & Glitter.

**The Creation of Gloopy & Glitter Animations**

Our vision for Gloopy & Glitter was to create a series of animations that could engage visitors in a way that static visuals and even the whimsical "Magnetic" series could not. We wanted these characters to embark on adventures, encounter mishaps, and most importantly, interact with each other and their environment in ways that evoke happiness, curiosity, and even the occasional belly laugh.

The animation studio we partnered with worked closely with our team to understand the essence of Silverburn and how Gloopy & Glitter could best represent that essence. Through countless sketches, storyboards, and iterations, we've developed a series of animations that we believe captures the magic of Silverburn while also propelling us into a new era of digital storytelling.

**Looking Forward**

As Gloopy & Glitter's animated antics begin to grace our screens, we close the chapter on the "Magnetic" idents with gratitude for the joy and wonder they brought to so many of our visitors. This transition symbolizes not just a change in visual strategy but also our commitment to evolving with our community, embracing innovation, and continuing to provide an unparalleled shopping experience.

We invite you to join Gloopy & Glitter in their new adventures throughout Silverburn. Their playful escapades are more than just entertainment – they're a reflection of the liveliness, creativity, and community spirit that makes Silverburn unique.
